ItacPersonItem.Notes: The Umoristi celebrated his funeral - See Gassendi, Viris illustris... Peiresc Vita (Paris: Cramoisy,1641), pp. 399-402. Useful resource is the Bibliotheque virtuelle Peiresc, on e-corpus: http://www.e-corpus.org/fre/virtualcollections/peiresc/notices/index.html. Peiresc`s portrait is in Gassendi (1641). Peiresc weas responsible for the affiliation to the Umoristi of French scholars, according to Gravit (1934). In 1621, Virginio Cesarini proposed Peiresc`s affiliation as Linceo. It is not clear whether he was formally associated to the Academy.
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
